summary: The main claim is: Llama2 was trained on dangerous information unsuitable for the application of being a 'chatbot'.
In truth, even releasing the open-source open-weights model at all, even if it hadn't been trained on dangerous info, would be bad.

The main claim is: However, I have tried to be critical of the fact that much of the progress in mechanistic interpretability research has been on “streetlight interpretability” projects often with cherrypicked models and tasks.
Benchmarks for this might involve using interpretability tools to reconstruct the function that was used to design or supervise a network.

top_comment.author: dr_s
top_comment.text: Something I'm curious about from anyone involved with the challenge - are there any standard tools to perform techinques like ablation on machine learning models developed in common frameworks, especially Pytorch? I often get the sense that the differential in development between capabilities and interpretability is reflected first and foremost in the tools available - plenty of libraries to design and train models relatively easily, but little in the way of interpretation unless you are able and willing to make your own tools. Am I wrong?
top_comment.claim: The comment's point: Something I'm curious about from anyone involved with the challenge - are there any standard tools to perform techinques like ablation on machine learning models developed in common frameworks, especially Pytorch?

summary: The main claim is: But since the generators are alien to the human, understanding them is harder than understanding non-alien generators.
Either generally-human-level AI will work internally like humans work internally, or not.
The main claim is: What could it mean to respect the boundaries of human and AI entities?

The main claim is: The fundamental behavior of GPT-3 is token prediction, which can straightforwardly be leveraged into text completion; in contrast, the fundamental behavior of InstructGPT is instruction following.
Instruction following is a new capability that uses the knowledge from the token prediction task to produce output as well as to understand input; how does that capability develop?

The main claim is: Shard theory makes a weak claim of alignment by default, and the strong claim that shard theoretic agents will be easily partially aligned to their human overseers (human-like values will get a seat at the shard negotiating table).

The main claim is: The following simple examples are to emphasize that there is not a straightforward intuitive relationship that says "when the Jacobian has less than full rank, there are fewer directions in parameter space along which the behaviour changes…
So we conclude that in a neighbourhood of a point in parameter space at which the Jacobian of F has full rank, the behaviour manifold is an (N−kD)-dimensional smooth submanifold.
The main claim is: Because, although the infant lacks any wisdom, it is also extremely small and weak, and so it poses no risk to the parent.
So in other words, the infant starts life with all three of these components (physicality, intellect, and wisdom) at very small, non-zero values.
The main claim is: There are therefore quite clear reasons for me why it would be a good idea to start this project which is why I'm wondering if there are any such plans.

The main claim is: Therefore, there will often be a tradeoff between pursuing direct and indirect benefits, especially when Benefits are not profit-maximizing (which by hypothesis they are not for the sake of this blog series).
This is a post in a series on "AI Benefits." It is cross-posted from my personal blog.
